name: NoteLift
shortDescription: Chrome extension to extract highlighted text from PDFs/webpages into modular revision notes locally.
problem: Students need a simple, zero-friction way to extract highlights from PDFs and webpages without logging in or using external cloud servers.
whyBuilt: Built to solve student revision collection friction. NoteLift acts as a private, local-first workflow extension.
contribution: Conceptualized logic and architecture independently; AI-assisted syntax level implementation.
challenges: Architecting reliable text-selection capture across nested iframes/PDF views and a modular folder-like note-organization system.
lessonsLearned: Learnt how to structure isolated extension context scripts, handle content-to-background messages, and optimize local storage footprint.

name: Brainstormzz
shortDescription: AI-powered brainstorming whiteboard turning prompts into structured, expandable idea categories.
problem: Visualizing complex prompt outputs as connected maps of raw ideas, avoiding simple flat text lists.
whyBuilt: Originally built for a Chrome AI Hackathon using Chrome's on-device Gemini Nano, later refactored for cloud APIs.
contribution: Full UI/whiteboard concept and design; implementation AI-assisted.
challenges: Getting accurate AI output and keeping generated ideas on-topic and reliably structured as JSON.
lessonsLearned: Discovered the impact of prompt engineering on JSON output schema consistency and Canvas render updates.

name: AI Career Engine
shortDescription: Dual-mode career discovery and resume-to-job optimization platform.
problem: Aligning resume skill sets and personality quizzes with actual role profiles, highlighting gaps and roadmap items.
whyBuilt: Built to provide candidates with structured roadmap directions based on raw resume analysis.
challenges: Resume-to-role matching/scoring logic (50% skill overlap + 50% personality signal alignment).
lessonsLearned: Learnt how to extract text from multi-page PDFs client-side and manage Express backend files flow using Multer.
futureImprovements: Add article and YouTube video suggestions into the roadmap output.

name: SSB Practice Simulator
shortDescription: Simulates WAT, PPDT, and SRT tests with official timing for Indian Armed Forces aspirants.
problem: Services Selection Board (SSB) candidates need realistic, timed mock environments for psychometric testing.
whyBuilt: Provide aspirants a free, high-fidelity practice simulator mimicking actual timing structures.
contribution: Built the timer logic and overall test-flow accuracy; AI-assisted on other parts.
challenges: Building the image bank for PPDT (no-repeat randomization).
lessonsLearned: Improved timed interval rendering state machines in React and viewport keyboard-shortcuts handlers.
futureImprovements: Add an AI-driven mock interviewer feature.

name: InstaSave Navigator
shortDescription: Chrome extension to instantly jump to the oldest saved Instagram posts.
problem: Instagram lacks a native way to navigate directly to the oldest saved posts, requiring endless manual scrolling.
whyBuilt: Built to solve navigation issues in massive saved lists.
challenges: Instagram's anti-automation scripts flagged automated scrolling and blocked operations.
lessonsLearned: Discovered the strict anti-scraping rules of major social platforms and Manifest V3 message constraints.
futureImprovements: None unless platform-detection workarounds are discovered.

name: RailDost
shortDescription: Visualizes direct vs via routes between railway stations on an interactive map.
problem: Comparing route choices and visual paths for Indian Railway journeys lacks visual comparison layers.
whyBuilt: Built to compare railway journey times visually on map layers.
challenges: Lack of stable public railway APIs; the limited-data API found was later pulled by its owner.
lessonsLearned: Learnt how to render map layers, draw polyline vectors, and handle mock fallback datasets.
futureImprovements: Replace hardcoded data with a real data source if one becomes public.
